stem: A release enters the software release pipeline. The probability of passing code review is 89%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing unit testing is 78%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing integration testing is between 60% and 72% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the release is deployed without incidents?

ground_truth.answer: P(deployed without incidents) ranges from 0.417 to 0.5 as the integration testing pass rate varies from 60% to 72%. Point estimate: 0.458.

solution_steps: The release must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the integration testing stage creates a 8.3 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the integration testing rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
